properties.name: Community Information
properties.location: between the townships of  Irrewillipe, Kawarren, Lovat, Wimba, Charleys Creek, Carlisle River and covers the township of Gellibrand
properties.webBody:
This Community Information message is being issued for between the townships of Irrewillipe, Kawarren, Lovat, Wimba, Charleys Creek, Carlisle River and covers the township of Gellibrand. 
  • There is a bushfire at Carlisle River that is under control.
  • This Community Information replaces the message issued on 13 March 2026.
  • Firefighters are currently patrolling this fire.
  • Please observe all track closures. 
There is currently no threat to the community however be aware of crews and heavy machinery working to rehabilitate affected tracks and stabilising areas of the fire ground.
This message was issued by Forest Fire Management Victoria.


Use multiple sources to stay informed: 
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfires).
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility:
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency.
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline.  
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact National Relay Service for assistance and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.
properties.text:
COMMUNITY INFORMATION - BUSHFIRE - Stay Informed
Incident Location: Carlisle River
Issue Date: Friday 20 March 2026, 11:15 am
Next Update: Friday 03 April 2026, 05:00 pm

More details at http://emergency.vic.gov.au/respond/#!/warning/41711/moreinfo

This Community Information message is being issued for between the townships of  Irrewillipe, Kawarren, Lovat, Wimba, Charleys Creek, Carlisle River and covers the township of Gellibrand. 
- There is a bushfire at Carlisle River that is under control.
- This Community Information replaces the message issued on 13 March 2026.
- Firefighters are currently patrolling this fire.
- Please observe all track closures. 


There is currently no threat to the community however be aware of crews and heavy machinery working to rehabilitate affected tracks and stabilising areas of the fire ground.

properties.name: Community Information
properties.webBody:
This Community Information message is being issued for Kanumbra. 
  • There is a haystack fire at Mcguigans Rd, Kanumbra that is under control.
  • Smoke will visible from nearby communities and along Maroondah Hwy. 
There is no immediate threat to the community and no action is required.
This message was issued by Country Fire Authority.

Unless the situation changes, this will be the last message issued for this fire.

Use multiple sources to stay informed: 
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226
  • VicEmergency app
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfires).
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility:
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency.
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call VicEmergency Hotline.  
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact National Relay Service for assistance and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

properties.name: Community Information
properties.webBody:
This Community Information is being issued for Drouin South, Lardner .
  • There is a fire involving multiple hay bales in a paddock at Carroll Rd, Drouin South. 
  • Haystack fires are not likely to spread into grassland but do generate a lot of smoke. 
  • Smoke may be visible from nearby roads and communities as firefighters pull apart the hay to extinguish the fire. 
  • Due to minimal wind in the local area, smoke haze and smell may be present and drifting through the area including Lardner Park which is currently host to the Farm World exhibition.  
There is no immediate threat to the community and no action is required.
This message was issued by Country Fire Authority.

Unless the situation changes, this will be the last message issued for this fire.

Use multiple sources to stay informed: 

  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au.
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226.
  • VicEmergency app.
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfires).
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility:
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency. 
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to cal VicEmergency
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact National Relay Service for assistance and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

COMMUNITY INFORMATION - GRASS FIRE - Stay Informed
Incident Location: Carroll Rd, Drouin South
Issue Date: Thursday 26 March 2026, 11:55 am
Next Update: Thursday 26 March 2026, 04:00 pm

More details at http://emergency.vic.gov.au/respond/#!/warning/41744/moreinfo

This Community Information is being issued for Drouin South, Lardner .
- There is a fire involving multiple hay bales in a paddock at Carroll Rd, Drouin South. 
- Haystack fires are not likely to spread into grassland but do generate a lot of smoke. 
- Smoke may be visible from nearby roads and communities as firefighters pull apart the hay to extinguish the fire. 
- Due to minimal wind in the local area, smoke haze and smell may be present and drifting through the area including Lardner Park which is currently host to the Farm World exhibition.  


There is no immediate threat to the community and no action is required.

properties.name: Community Information
properties.webBody:
This Community Information is being issued for Wilby and surrounds.
  • There is a haystack fire at Lavis Road, Wilby. 
  • Haystack fires are not likely to spread into grassland but do generate a lot of smoke.
  • Smoke may be visible from nearby roads and communities for the next 3 days.
  • Firefighters are monitoring this fire.
There is no immediate threat to the community and no action is required.
This message was issued by Country Fire Authority.

Unless the situation changes, this will be the last message issued for this fire.

Use multiple sources to stay informed: 

  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au.
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226.
  • VicEmergency app.
  • Facebook or Twitter (#vicfires).
  • Tune in to ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.
Accessibility:
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency. 
  • To access this information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to cal VicEmergency
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact National Relay Service for assistance and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.

properties.webHeadline:
DAMAGING SOUTHERLY WIND GUSTS with peak gusts of around 90 km/h are expected to develop over parts of the central ranges and east on Friday morning. Wind gusts may reach 100 km/h about the East Gippsland coast.
